sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.1g of fat. saturated fat and omega3 matter most here. for a tuber, this mostly lives or dies on carb behaviour.
sections.subtitleText: 0.1g of fat. saturated fat and omega3 matter most here. for a tuber, this mostly lives or dies on carb behaviour.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 17.5g of carbs. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
sections.subtitleText: 17.5g of carbs. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 2g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
sections.subtitleText: 2g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin c at 22% daily value stands out most. that is only mild support overall.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin c at 22% daily value stands out most. that is only mild support overall.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: minerals are basically not part of the case here.
sections.subtitleText: minerals are basically not part of the case here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: very practical staple food because cheap, common, and easy to build meals around, which matters for a tuber category built on energy foods. can be fairly filling because especially when eaten in less processed forms. brings some useful potassium because not amazing, but enough to matter a bit in the category.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: glycemic behaviour is only middling because not as clean as the best tubers on the carb-behaviour axis. fibre is only modest because decent, but not category-leading. micronutrient return is fine rather than elite because strong enough to be respectable, not enough to be exceptional.

displayItems.title: micronutrient return is fine rather than elite
displayItems.explanation: Strong enough to be respectable, not enough to be exceptional.

sections.subtitleText: now the downsides: glycemic behaviour is only middling because not as clean as the best tubers on the carb-behaviour axis. fibre is only modest because decent, but not category-leading. micronutrient return is fine rather than elite because strong enough to be respectable, not enough to be exceptional.

closing.summary: White Potato has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in A tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in A tier with an overall score of 46 from a base nutrition score of 42. Context lifted it by 4 points.
